entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: The legume crop studied for aflatoxin contamination and drought tolerance.

entities.name: AFLATOXIN B1 CONTAMINATION
entities.description: The contamination of peanuts by Aflatoxin B1 produced by Aspergillus flavus.

entities.name: DROUGHT STRESS
entities.description: Environmental condition characterized by lack of water available to the plant.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of peanut genotypes to maintain yield and resist contamination under water-stressed conditions.

entities.name: ASPERGILLUS FLAVUS
entities.description: The fungus responsible for colonization and aflatoxin production in peanuts.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The general geographic region where the research is applicable.

entities.name: ICRISAT SAHELIAN CENTER
entities.description: The specific location where the experiments were conducted.

entities.name: INTERMITTENT WATER-STRESSED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Water-stressed conditions imposed from the 60th day after sowing to maturity.

entities.name: WELL-WATERED CONDITIONS
entities.description: Control conditions where plants received adequate water.

entities.name: ALPHA LATTICE DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used to assess the genotypes.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The measure of pod, seed, and haulm production of the peanut plant.

entities.name: SEED COAT TOTAL POLYPHENOL
entities.description: A biochemical marker in the seed coat associated with resistance to aflatoxin contamination.

entities.name: IMMATURE PODS PER PLANT
entities.description: The number of pods that fail to mature, often increasing under drought stress.

relationships.description: Causes contamination through colonization and infection
Genotypes of this crop are evaluated for this trait
Drought-tolerant genotypes show resistance to contamination
Significant relationship between this marker and aflatoxin resistance
Reduces pod, seed, and haulm yield
Increases the number of immature pods
Used as the experimental framework to assess peanut genotypes
Location where peanut experiments were carried out
